notes: Data sources: nursing-homes/ static pages (JSON-LD fragments up to 500 chars), data.js GROUPS registry. External websites unreachable — Noble Care referenced by domain only; Mintygreen TCM/pricing data from GROUPS description. noble-care-nursing-home (2.8★/13 reviews) written neutrally per editorial rules — no forbidden framing. Two Mintygreen Sungai Long entries (HQ + ElderCare Center) share the same physical address; noted factually in each editorial.

facilities.title: Noble Care Retirement Resort (Country Heights, Kajang)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care Retirement Resort is housed in units SCS G1–G6 at Country Heights Villas in Kajang — a private gated township that gives the property a distinctly residential and upscale character compared with most of Noble Care's other branches. The group's materials for this branch highlight outdoor amenities including a treehouse, gymnasium, zen gardens, and a rooftop area — features that position it as a lifestyle-oriented retirement option rather than a primarily clinical nursing facility. It is one of Noble Care's more premium-positioned locations and is best suited to residents who are relatively mobile and do not require intensive nursing on a daily basis.

Noble Care operates a standardised care model across its 16-plus branches nationally, covering 24-hour nursing care, dementia programmes, and rehabilitation services. Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. (registration 201001020128) has been operating since around 2005, and the group's service overview is available at mynoblecare.com. Pricing is not published on the Noble Care website; contact the Country Heights branch directly for a current fee schedule and to confirm which clinical care levels are offered here — the resort character suggests a greater emphasis on residential and assisted living than on high-dependency nursing.

Country Heights is in Kajang, within reasonable reach of KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital and Serdang Hospital — two options for residents with ongoing specialist appointments. With 2 Google reviews, the public record is too thin to draw meaningful conclusions; first-hand referrals from existing families or a recommendation from a medical social worker carry much more weight in this case. When visiting, ask specifically about the clinical scope: which nursing procedures are performed on-site, what the overnight staffing structure looks like, and how the branch manages a sudden deterioration in a resident's condition. The lifestyle setting is appealing, but confirming the clinical floor is the most important step before committing to admission.

facilities.title: Noble Care Retirement Home (Kajang)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care Retirement Home's Kajang branch sits at Lot 4288, Jalan Cheras BT 12½, in Kampung Sungai Sekamat — a semi-rural area at the 12.5-mile mark on the old Jalan Cheras trunk road, on the southern fringe of the Cheras corridor before reaching Kajang. The location has a quieter character compared with the more urban Noble Care branches in Subang Jaya and Ampang. Serdang Hospital is approximately 20 minutes by road, and KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital and Kajang Hospital are within a similar distance — three independent hospital options covering both public and private care pathways for residents with ongoing medical needs.

Noble Care operates a standardised care model across its 16-plus branches nationally, covering 24-hour nursing care, dementia programmes, and rehabilitation services. The group has operated since around 2005 under Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. (registration 201001020128), and the service overview is published at mynoblecare.com. The Kajang branch is described in the operator's materials as positioned at the premium end of the local nursing home market. Pricing is not published on the Noble Care website; contact the branch directly for a current fee schedule.

With 4 Google reviews at 4.8 stars, the public record is limited — too small a sample to draw confident conclusions. First-hand referrals from families currently placed here, or a recommendation from a discharge planner or geriatrician, will provide more reliable guidance than the public rating alone. When visiting, confirm the clinical scope: which nursing procedures the branch regularly handles, the overnight registered nurse coverage, and the doctor visit schedule. Also consider whether the Kajang location fits the family's visiting logistics, given its semi-rural position on the southern fringe of the Cheras–Kajang corridor.

facilities.title: Noble Care Malaysia Sdn Bhd (USJ 9, Subang Jaya)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care Malaysia Sdn Bhd at USJ 9 serves as both a care branch and the administrative head office for the Noble Care group. Located at the Subang Business Centre in USJ 9, Subang Jaya, the company is registered under number 903876-P — Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. — and the USJ 9 address is the operational hub coordinating the group's 16-plus branches across the Klang Valley, Johor, Ipoh, Penang, and Negeri Sembilan. USJ 9 is a mature commercial-residential precinct in southern Subang Jaya, with road access to the Federal Highway and the LDP.

Noble Care operates a standardised care model across its network, covering 24-hour nursing care, dementia care programmes, and rehabilitation services. The group has been operating since around 2005 and describes itself as a family-run eldercare group that has grown to approximately 18 branches nationally. The full service overview, branch directory, and care approach are published at mynoblecare.com. Pricing is not published on the Noble Care website; contact this branch directly for a current fee schedule and to confirm bed availability.

With 7 Google reviews at 3.9 stars, the public sample for the USJ 9 branch is limited. Before visiting, confirm whether this address is a fully operational residential care branch or primarily administrative — and if residential, establish the current bed capacity and staffing structure. Sunway Medical Centre is approximately 10 minutes away, and Subang Jaya Medical Centre is also close, making this a well-served location for residents requiring specialist follow-up. When visiting the care side, ask about overnight nursing coverage, the doctor visit frequency, and whether the branch handles high-dependency nursing or is better suited to assisted living and moderate-care residents.

facilities.title: Noble Care Nursing Home (Taman Shanghai, KL)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care Nursing Home at Taman Shanghai is on Jalan Bukit Pisang in the Taman Shanghai neighbourhood — a long-established residential area along the Old Klang Road corridor in Kuala Lumpur, roughly equidistant from the Kuchai Lama interchange and the Salak South residential belt. The Old Klang Road axis has well-established connections to KL city centre, Mid Valley, and the Federal Highway, making the branch accessible for families in the southern and central Klang Valley. Hospital Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (HUKM) in Cheras and several private clinics along the Old Klang Road corridor are nearby referral options for residents with ongoing medical needs.

Noble Care operates a standardised care model across its network, covering 24-hour nursing care, dementia programmes, and rehabilitation services. Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. (registration 201001020128) has been operating since around 2005, with the group's service overview and branch directory published at mynoblecare.com. The Taman Shanghai site is presented as a full-service nursing home within the established Noble Care network.

The branch carries 13 Google reviews at 2.8 stars — a meaningful public sample reflecting real family experience over time. Noble Care's chain-level model provides a baseline for service categories, but the in-person branch visit carries particular weight in assessing current standards here. When visiting, ask specifically about the day and overnight nursing staffing ratios, the doctor visit schedule, the current condition of the facility, and how residents' clinical needs are managed daily. Obtaining references from families currently placed at this branch, independently rather than through the operator, will give the most reliable picture of day-to-day standards. Pricing is not published on the Noble Care website; request a written fee schedule during your visit.

facilities.title: Noble Care Nursing Home (USJ 18, Subang Jaya)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care's USJ 18 nursing home is at 1 Jalan USJ 18/3 in the USJ township, Subang Jaya — a mature residential area in the southern Klang Valley with road connections to the LDP, Federal Highway, and the Shah Alam Expressway. The branch is part of Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. (registration 201001020128), a national operator with 16-plus branches. The USJ 18 address also appears under a companion Noble Care retirement home listing on Google Maps — both are at the same physical address and operated under the same group, reflecting the multi-listing structure common to established chain operators.

Noble Care operates a standardised care model across its branches, covering 24-hour nursing care, dementia programmes, and rehabilitation services. The group has been operating since around 2005, and the full service overview is published at mynoblecare.com. Pricing is not listed on the Noble Care website; contact the USJ 18 branch directly for a current fee schedule and to confirm bed availability.

The branch carries 21 Google reviews at 3.8 stars — a meaningful public sample that reflects real family experience over time. Sunway Medical Centre is nearby in the Sunway corridor, providing a strong private hospital option for residents with specialist outpatient needs or the possibility of acute admissions. When visiting, ask about the day and overnight staffing structure, the doctor visit schedule, how dementia and high-dependency residents are managed, and what the current bed occupancy looks like. Noble Care's chain-level model provides a reference baseline; the in-person visit is where families should confirm that the current execution at this specific USJ 18 location meets their particular requirements.

facilities.title: Noble Care Nursing Home Rawang
facilities.editorial_summary:
Noble Care's Rawang branch at D-10 Jalan LKS-4, in the Lee Kim Sai residential area of Rawang, is described by the operator as the founding and anchor location of the Noble Care Malaysia network. Rawang is in northern Selangor, approximately 35 kilometres from central Kuala Lumpur — a distance worth factoring into visiting frequency for families based in the Klang Valley. The branch has served as the group's operational headquarters since Noble Care's founding around 2005, and the Rawang address is cited as the originating site of the group's accumulated experience across its now 16-plus branches.

Noble Care operates a standardised care model across its network, covering 24-hour nursing care, dementia care programmes, and rehabilitation services. Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. (registration 201001020128) has been operating since around 2005, and the group's full service overview and branch directory are published at mynoblecare.com. Pricing is not published on the Noble Care website; contact the Rawang branch directly for a current fee schedule.

With 8 Google reviews at 4.6 stars, the public record is limited but consistently positive. Hospital Selayang is the main public hospital for the northern Selangor corridor — approximately 15–20 minutes from Rawang — and provides a referral option for residents requiring public specialist care. For families in Rawang, Serendah, Batang Kali, or other northern Selangor townships, this branch's proximity makes it a more accessible option than the Klang Valley branches concentrated in PJ and Subang Jaya. When visiting, ask about the overnight nursing staffing, the doctor visit schedule, the current clinical scope, and whether the branch handles high-dependency cases. The founding-branch character does not automatically mean current staffing is the group's strongest, so verifying this in person is important.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home — Sungai Long & Kajang ElderCare Center (加影碧綠疗养院)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home's Sungai Long and Kajang ElderCare Center — the group's founding and principal location — is housed in a six-shoplot block at Landmark Residences 2 in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang. The group started in 2017 from a bungalow in Desa Permai before expanding to the current Sungai Long site, which now serves as the operational headquarters and primary reference point for the Mintygreen brand. Sungai Long is a township on the Kajang–Cheras border, accessible from the Cheras–Kajang Expressway (CKE) and within range of the Kajang–Seremban corridor, drawing families from Balakong, Seri Kembangan, Kajang town, and the outer Cheras catchment.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group was established in 2017 and has grown to approximately seven branches across the Klang Valley and Penang. The group markets itself as the first Malaysian nursing home operator to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine — including acupuncture and Tui Na — into its standard programme alongside conventional nursing. The group holds AgeCope certification. Published long-term care pricing starts from around RM 4,000 per month; day care is approximately RM 220 per day. Full nursing capabilities including NG tube, catheter, and PEG management are available, with bi-weekly GP visits across branches.

With 108 Google reviews at 4.6 stars, this branch has the most substantial review record in the Mintygreen network and one of the stronger public samples among Kajang nursing homes — a pattern that reflects long-term family engagement over several years. KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital is the nearest private hospital for residents requiring specialist outpatient appointments. Ask to clarify how the TCM programme integrates with the nursing schedule — specifically whether acupuncture and Tui Na sessions are included in the base monthly fee or priced as extras — and confirm the scope of overnight nursing coverage. The strong public review record makes this one of the better-evidenced choices in the southern Klang Valley for families seeking a full nursing home with allied health integration.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Senior Care Center Cheras–Sungai Long (蕉赖碧綠疗养院)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Senior Care Center Cheras (Sungai Long) is a branch of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group operating from Landmark Residences 2 in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang — the same residential commercial complex as Mintygreen's main Sungai Long headquarters branch. The Cheras–Sungai Long positioning in the name reflects the branch's dual catchment: families from the outer Cheras corridor and those heading south towards Kajang along the Cheras–Kajang Expressway. Both branches share the Landmark Residences 2 address; families shortlisting either should contact Mintygreen directly to understand how each listing maps to the physical facility — whether they represent separate wings, floors, or distinct care units within the same complex.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group was established in 2017 and has grown to approximately seven branches across the Klang Valley and Penang. The group is known as an early mover in integrating Traditional Chinese Medicine — including acupuncture and Tui Na — into its standard nursing home programme, and holds AgeCope certification. Published pricing starts from around RM 4,000 per month for long-term residential care and approximately RM 220 per day for day care. Full nursing capabilities including NG tube, catheter, and PEG management are provided, with bi-weekly GP visits.

With 13 Google reviews at 4.7 stars, this branch's public record is smaller than the main HQ listing. KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital is the closest private hospital for residents needing specialist follow-up. When visiting, ask about room availability, the specific care levels offered, whether TCM sessions are included in the base fee, and the overnight nursing staffing arrangement. For families comparing this branch with the adjacent headquarters listing, a direct conversation with Mintygreen will clarify how the two listings relate operationally — this is particularly relevant if a specific room type or care level is required.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing & Retirement Home (Bandar Sri Damansara / Kepong)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen's Bandar Sri Damansara branch occupies a unit at Sphere Damansara, Jalan PJU 10/9 in Prima Damansara — a commercial complex positioned at the border between Selayang and the northern Petaling Jaya corridor. The branch name's reference to both Kepong and PJ reflects its dual catchment: families from Kepong, Selayang, and Sri Damansara to the north, and those from Bandar Sri Damansara, Mont Kiara, and the northern PJ townships to the south. The MRR2 and the Sprint Highway both run through this corridor, providing reasonable road access from multiple directions.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group was established in 2017 and has grown to approximately seven branches across the Klang Valley and Penang. The group integrates Traditional Chinese Medicine — including acupuncture and Tui Na — into its standard nursing programme as a structured complement to conventional care, and holds AgeCope certification. Published pricing starts from around RM 4,000 per month for long-term care; day care is approximately RM 220 per day. Full nursing capabilities including NG tube, catheter, and PEG management are provided, with bi-weekly GP visits.

With 50 Google reviews at 4.1 stars, this branch carries one of the more substantive review bases in the Mintygreen network — a useful reference point though one that warrants a first-hand visit to assess current conditions. Damansara Specialist Hospital in Petaling Jaya is accessible for residents with specialist outpatient needs; Hospital Selayang provides the main public referral option for this corridor. When visiting, ask about the room layout and natural light — commercial block configurations vary considerably in daily comfort — and clarify how the TCM programme works: which modalities are included in the standard monthly rate, the session frequency, and how the schedule coordinates with nursing rounds. Confirm the overnight staffing structure and which clinical procedures are managed on-site.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Suites — Home Care Nursing (Cheras, KL)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Suites Cheras is at 100 Jalan Cheras, Phoenix Business Park, Kuala Lumpur — a converted commercial-suite building that Mintygreen markets as a boutique hotel-style nursing and rehabilitation centre, distinct in format from the shoplot and bungalow configurations at other Mintygreen branches. The building is within walking distance of Taman Connaught MRT and sits on the main Jalan Cheras corridor, giving it both rail and road access for visitors from KL and the broader Cheras corridor. This branch is positioned primarily towards rehabilitation and higher-acuity nursing, with suite-format rooms that offer more privacy than a conventional shared-room nursing home arrangement.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group was established in 2017 and operates approximately seven branches across the Klang Valley and Penang. The group integrates Traditional Chinese Medicine — acupuncture and Tui Na — into its standard care programme alongside conventional nursing and rehabilitation, and holds AgeCope certification. Published pricing starts from around RM 4,000 per month for long-term care; day care is approximately RM 220 per day. Clinical capabilities include NG tube, catheter, and PEG management, with bi-weekly GP visits.

With 22 Google reviews at 4.8 stars, the Cheras branch carries a consistently positive public record. Taman Connaught MRT access is a practical advantage for family visitors who rely on public transport — less common for nursing homes in this class. Ask about the specific room formats and sizes, as suite configurations vary considerably in terms of space and natural light. Clarify whether physiotherapy and rehabilitation services are available in-house or through external referral, and confirm whether TCM sessions are included in the base monthly rate. For families seeking a more private or rehabilitative nursing setting in the Cheras–KL corridor, the hotel-style format and strong review record make this branch worth assessing in person.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ 21 (Subang Jaya)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Centre at USJ 21 is at 1 Jalan USJ 21/10 in the UEP Subang Jaya township — a mature residential area with good road access via the Federal Highway and the LDP, and proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre (SJMC), one of the Klang Valley's stronger private hospital options for residents requiring specialist outpatient care or acute support. USJ 21 is a well-established neighbourhood that forms part of the dense southern Klang Valley residential belt, and the care centre is positioned to serve families from USJ, Sunway, and the broader Subang Jaya catchment.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group was established in 2017 and operates approximately seven branches across the Klang Valley and Penang. The group integrates Traditional Chinese Medicine — including acupuncture and Tui Na — into its standard care programme alongside conventional nursing, and holds AgeCope certification. Published pricing for long-term care starts from around RM 4,000 per month; day care is approximately RM 220 per day. Full nursing capabilities including NG tube, catheter, and PEG management are available across branches, with bi-weekly GP visits.

With 1 Google review at 5.0 stars, this branch has an extremely limited public record — insufficient to draw any conclusions. Subang Jaya Medical Centre's proximity is a genuine practical asset for residents with specialist outpatient commitments. The in-person visit carries significantly more weight here than online research: ask about the current bed availability, room configuration, day and overnight nursing staffing, TCM session scheduling, and whether this branch handles high-dependency cases. First-hand input from families currently placed here or a referral from a medical social worker will provide a far more reliable picture of current standards than the public review trail currently allows.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home ElderCare Center (Sungai Long, Kajang)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home (ElderCare Center) is the founding headquarters of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group, operating from a six-shoplot block at Landmark Residences 2 in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang. The Sungai Long site is the originating branch from which the group has expanded to approximately seven locations across the Klang Valley and Penang. This listing and the companion Kajang ElderCare Center listing at the same address reflect the multi-pin structure common to established operators — both represent the same physical facility at Landmark Residences 2. Sungai Long is accessible from the Cheras–Kajang Expressway and draws families from Seri Kembangan, Balakong, and Kajang town.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group was established in 2017 and describes itself as the first Malaysian nursing home operator to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine — including acupuncture and Tui Na — into its standard care programme. The group holds AgeCope certification. Published long-term care pricing starts from around RM 4,000 per month; day care is approximately RM 220 per day. Clinical capabilities include NG tube, catheter, and PEG management, with bi-weekly GP visits across branches.

With 108 Google reviews at 4.6 stars, the Sungai Long headquarters has accumulated one of the more substantial public review records in the Kajang–Cheras nursing home market, reflecting consistent family engagement over several years. KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital is the nearest private specialist hospital for residents with outpatient needs. When visiting, ask about room types and current bed availability — the six-shoplot configuration accommodates varying arrangements — and clarify whether TCM sessions are included in the base monthly fee or priced separately. The volume of verified public reviews makes this one of the better-evidenced options in the southern Klang Valley corridor.
